summary: Seven gradient fallback values are duplicated verbatim across ::before and ::after selectors, differing only in border-box vs padding-box, creating ~30 lines of identical CSS.
failure_scenario: Define the fallback gradients as CSS custom properties scoped to the card (e.g. --gradient-one-fallback, --gradient-two-fallback, ...) and reference only the custom property in both selectors, eliminating the duplicate inline fallbacks.

summary: Four preview sections (Featured, Editorial, Memory, City) in ImageVaultOverview repeat the identical ScrollReveal/title/desc/grid/view-all-link structure with only data differences.
failure_scenario: Define a previewSections array mapping category to { items, title, desc, href } and render them with a single map() call and a reusable PreviewSection component, eliminating 4 structurally identical blocks.

summary: The inline ternary expression for item zoom class (item.id === 'editorial-004' / 'illustration-004') is duplicated at both ImageCategoryView and ImageVaultOverview locations.
failure_scenario: Extract a module-level helper function, e.g. `function getItemZoomClass(id: string): string`, that returns the correct modifier suffix, and call it in both places.

summary: Six BorderGlow props (backgroundColor, borderRadius, glowRadius, glowIntensity, coneSpread, edgeSensitivity) are repeated verbatim across 6 instances in ArchiveNotesPage, plus ~9 more in ArchivePage, ArchiveProjectsPage, and About — only glowColor and colors vary per instance.
failure_scenario: Set edgeSensitivity default to 40 and coneSpread default to 10 in BorderGlow's props (or create an ArchiveBorderGlow wrapper with these defaults), then pass only the varying props (className, glowColor, colors) at each call site.

summary: window.scrollTo(0,0) fires on every hashchange to a non-home page, including transitions between nested archive sub-routes (e.g. Notes root -> Note category), causing unwanted scroll position resets within sub-pages.
failure_scenario: Track the previous top-level page segment and only call scrollTo(0,0) when the top-level page changes (e.g. home -> archive, not archive/images -> archive/images/featured), letting individual pages handle their own scroll restoration.

summary: visualArchiveItems.slice(-3).reverse() implicitly assumes items are always appended at array end in chronological order — if items are ever reordered, inserted mid-array, or filtered, the 'recent additions' will be wrong.
failure_scenario: Add an optional dateAdded or sortOrder field to each VisualArchiveItem and sort by it explicitly, or extract the newest N items via a stable ordering key rather than relying on array position.
